items.definition_en: A bus is a large vehicle that carries many people.
items.example_en: We took the bus to school.

items.definition_en: A train is a long vehicle that runs on tracks.
items.example_en: He waits for the train every morning.

items.definition_en: A tram is a street car that travels on rails.
items.example_en: The tram stopped at the corner.

items.definition_en: A subway is an underground train.
items.example_en: She rides the subway to work.

items.definition_en: A taxi is a car you pay to take you somewhere.
items.example_en: He called a taxi after the party.

items.definition_en: A ferry is a boat that carries people or cars.
items.example_en: We crossed the river on a ferry.

items.definition_en: A coach is a large bus for long trips.
items.example_en: The tourists traveled by coach.

items.definition_en: A minibus is a small bus for a few people.
items.example_en: We took a minibus to the airport.

items.definition_en: A shuttle is a vehicle that goes back and forth.
items.example_en: The hotel offers a shuttle to the station.

items.definition_en: A rickshaw is a small two-wheeled vehicle pulled by a person.
items.example_en: We took a rickshaw through the city streets.

items.definition_en: A trolleybus is an electric bus using wires above the street.
items.example_en: They took the trolleybus downtown.

items.definition_en: A commuter train takes people between their home and work.
items.example_en: He takes a commuter train every weekday.

items.definition_en: A double-decker is a tall bus with two levels.
items.example_en: London is famous for its red double-deckers.

items.definition_en: A monorail is a train running on a single rail.
items.example_en: The monorail takes visitors around the park.

items.definition_en: Light rail is a fast city passenger train.
items.example_en: The city introduced a new light rail line.

items.definition_en: A funicular is a train that goes up a steep hill.
items.example_en: We took the funicular to the top of the hill.

items.definition_en: A cable car moves on rails by pulling cables.
items.example_en: We rode the cable car in San Francisco.

items.definition_en: A city bus is a public bus in a town or city.
items.example_en: The city bus stops near my house.

items.definition_en: A night bus is a bus that travels late at night.
items.example_en: I took the night bus home after midnight.

items.definition_en: An express train travels faster and stops less often.
items.example_en: The express train got us there quickly.
